title: Juanulloa parviflora
intro: Juanulloa parviflora is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the family Solanaceae and known for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Juanulloa parviflora include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as capsules and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
